title: Sign Up Page (`app/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx`)
content:
This file defines the sign-up page component for the DocMed application, utilizing Clerk for authentication and Next.js for routing and rendering.

## Purpose

Provides a user interface for new users to create an account. It handles both the initial sign-up with email and password, and the subsequent email verification process. It also includes an option for Google OAuth sign-up.

## Key Exports and Functions

*   **`SignUpPage` Component:** The main React component for the sign-up page.
    *   **State Variables:** Manages `email`, `password`, `code`, `pendingVerification`, `error`, and `loading` states.
    *   **`handleSubmit`:** Asynchronous function to create a new user account with Clerk and initiate email verification.
    *   **`handleVerify`:** Asynchronous function to attempt email verification using the provided code.
    *   **`handleGoogleSignUp`:** Initiates the Google OAuth sign-up flow.
    *   **`formFields`:** Configuration object for the `AnimatedForm` component, defining labels, types, placeholders, and event handlers for input fields.

## Components Used

*   **`@clerk/nextjs/legacy`:** Provides `useSignUp` hook for authentication logic.
*   **`next/navigation`:** Provides `useRouter` for navigation.
*   **`next/link`:** For creating navigation links.
*   **UI Components (from `@/components/ui/modern-animated-sign-in`)**:
    *   `AnimatedForm`
    *   `Ripple`
    *   `TechOrbitDisplay`
    *   `BoxReveal`
    *   `Input`
    *   `Label`
*   **Icons (from `lucide-react`)**: `FileText`, `Brain`, `Shield`, `Sparkles`, `RefreshCw`, `Zap`, `GitBranch`, `Code2`.

## Usage Example

When a user navigates to `/sign-up`, this component will render. Initially, they will see fields for email and password. Upon submission, if successful, the UI will transition to prompt for an email verification code. A "Continue with Google" button is also available for alternative sign-up.
